title: Flight Attendant

rationales.taskAutomation: The role is dominated by in-person passenger service, physical safety demonstrations, and emergency response, none of which current AI can perform end-to-end.
rationales.toolMaturity: No shipped product automates cabin safety or service duties; AI is limited to back-office scheduling and booking tools that don't touch the in-cabin role.
rationales.adoption: Airlines use AI for crew scheduling and booking systems, not for any part of the physical in-flight role.
rationales.agenticExposure: In-person safety command and physical presence keep this role outside current agentic reach.

tasksAtRisk: Routine in-app passenger notifications and rebooking prompts
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 53-2031, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Emergency response and evacuation command certification

insight: This is an in-person safety role first and a service role second; current AI has no path to performing either at the required reliability, so displacement risk stays low regardless of how much AI improves in adjacent booking and scheduling systems.
